content.full_text:
November 20, 1990


Attn: Wayne Imberi
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 007
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

I appreciate your help during our telephone conversation last week concerning campaign expenditures. I have taken on the job of assisting my husband to understand which forms are necessary for this purpose and to help provide him the financial and other information necessary for same. Therefore, at his request, I called for your position regarding informational publishing in the local advertiser, the Desert Mailer, in which he intends to get out information regarding his concern over an upcoming consideration of a rate raise increase. He will be paying $109.01 out of his own money.

It is my understanding of that conversation, that the information article to the District customers which my husband paid for (copy of text submitted to the paper attached) is not covered under campaign statutes and, therefore, does not require the filing of Forms 501 and 502 in the event that he 'does choose to run for that office again.

I'd like to thank you for your prompt response to our inquiry. I was prepared to place a stop payment on the check, open a campaign account with personal funds, file the forms and issue a new check if my husband felt that it would affect the possibility of his running for that office again. Your quick response that this article was not covered under the campaign statutes saved me that time, effort and confus': which that requirement would have caused.

It was my understanding from a representative from the Los Angeles County Campaign Reporting Section that I should get your answer writing. Therefore, in order to keep my records complete I would appreciate it if you would show your agreement to our understanding of the situation on the duplicate enclosed and returning it to me in the stamped self-addressed envelope provided or by advising of your position in writing and returning it to me for completion of my records.

Thank you for all your help in this matter.

[signature]

Eleanor Koppel
P.O. Box 4055
Lancaster, CA  93539

[illegible]
[illegible]

QUARTZ HILL WATER RATE INCREASE??

As a member of the board of directors of the quartz hill water district, i want to advise our users of my understanding that the board of directors will consider raising the water rates at the next regular meeting, december 6, 1990 at 7p.m. the location is the district office at 42141 54th st. west in quartz hill.

As i remember, last time the rates were increased, some of our users complained after the raise. The reason i am advising you of the rate raise consideration is so that those who do not want a rate increase can make their wishes known either before or during the meeting. BEFORE AN INCREASE TAKES PLACE I FEEL IS THE MOST EFFECTIVE TIME TO COMBAT IT.

I, MYSELF, DO NOT FEEL A RATE INCREASE IS INDICATED FOR THE FOLLOWING REASONS:

1. I understand that this raise is being considered because A.V.E.K is raising their rates to us. But to just what extent that rate increase will affect us will not actually be known for some time to come.

2. I felt that it was my mandate from the voters to assure that the water facilities needed for new development were paid for by the developers, not the existing District users. Therefore, I fought for effective developer fees which were finally set into place and, I am happy to suggest, in time to provide the finances for those facilities which will be needed for these new homes when sold and occupied. Those fees should effectively, i.e., keep the cost of these facilities from being a financial burden to existing users, which was my intent.

3. Accordingly, last year was, I believe, the most financially successful in the history of the District, providing excellent financial stability.

4. The District also sold an easement for electrical power lines across one of our tank sites. The easement was sold for more than our original cost of that property, thereby, as I believe the auditor expressed it, being a "windfall" to the District. As far as I am concerned, this "windfall" can more than offset the expected shortfall which the A.V.E.K. raise may cause the Quartz Hill Water District in 1991.

THEREFORE, I WILL GO TO THE MEETING PREPARED TO OFFER TWO MOTIONS:

1. That, due to the very healthy financial shape the District is in, NO RATE INCREASE BE INITIATED AT THIS TIME.

2. THAT THE NEXT CONSIDERATION OF A RATE INCREASE BE SET FOR THE DECEMBER MEETING IN 1991. Thus giving the board actual experience, to see, to just what extent the A.V.E.K. raise will really affect the District.

For those of you who wish to voice your opinions regarding the RATE INCREASE, I hope you and your friends will attend the meeting. If you cannot attend, but want to have input, you could write me as follows: Martin Koppel - Director, Quartz Hill Water District, 42141 50th St. West, Quartz Hill, CA 93536. If you wish more information, my home phone number is 943-4628. BUT I SUGGEST THAT ATTENDING THE MEETING WOULD PROVE MOST EFFECTIVE. Hope to see you there. (This information was presented and paid for by Martin Koppel.)

embedding.qa_text: November 20, 1990 Attn: Wayne Inberi Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 P.O. Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 I appreciate your help during our telephone conversation last week concerning campaign expenditures. I have taken on the job of assisting my husband to understand which forms are necessary for his response and to help provide him the financial and other information necessary for same. Therefore, at his request, I called for your position regarding the informational publishing in the local advertiser, The Desert Mailer, in which he intends to get out information regarding his concern over an upcoming consideration of a rate raise increase. He will be paying $109.01 out of his own money. It is my understanding of that conversation, that the information article to the District customers which my husband paid for (copy of text submitted to the paper attached) is not covered under campaign statutes and, therefore, does not require the filing of Forms 501 and 502 in the event that he does choose to run for that office again. I'd like to thank you for your prompt response to our inquiry. I was prepared to place a stop payment on the check, open a campaign account with personal funds, file the forms and issue a new check if my husband felt that it would affect the possibility of his running for that office again. Your quick response that this article was not covered under the campaign statutes saved me that time, effort and confusion which that requirement would have caused. It was my understanding from a representative from the Los Angeles County Campaign Reporting Section that I should get your answer in writing. Therefore, in order to keep my records complete I would appreciate it if you would show your agreement to our understanding of the situation on the duplicate enclosed and returning it to me in the stamped self-addressed envelope provided or by advising us of your position in writing and returning it to me for completion of my records. Thank you for all your help in this matter. Eleanor Koppel P.O. Box 4055 Lancaster, CA 93539 The image consists entirely of text, which appears to be in Italian. Due to the quality and formatting of the scan, some words and lines are partially cut off or extremely faint, making it difficult to transcribe accurately. Here is what I can reliably extract: "new real interessanti scoperte che grazie alle loro eccezionali caratteristiche non avevano mai avuto l'opportunità di essere esposte al pubblico. Questo nuovo approccio ai materiali antichi, che utilizza tecniche di analisi avanzate come la microscopia elettronica a scansione, ha permesso di ottenere risultati sorprendenti. In particolare, sono stati identificati nuovi elementi chimici nei resti umani rinvenuti nel sito di Neanderthal, confermando così l'ipotesi che questi esseri fossero più simili agli uomini moderni di quanto si pensasse fino ad ora." Translation: "New really interesting discoveries that, due to their exceptional characteristics, had never had the opportunity to be exposed to the public.
